mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real GDP growth and a decline in retail sales volume signals aggregate demand weakness that reduces corporate revenue expectations, driving negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, an increase in industrial production indicates supply-side resilience that offsets higher borrowing costs, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive skew when INDPRO rises and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a sharp deceleration in construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline below threshold but SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in payroll employment growth and a simultaneous rise in the federal funds rate reflects a labor market that can absorb higher borrowing costs without triggering layoffs, supporting positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS fails to predict positive SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the consumer price index erodes real purchasing power and forces central bank hawkishness, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL rises consecutively for three months while SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds 250,000 jobs for two consecutive months, robust labor demand sustains consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 250000 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth exceeds threshold but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances.
